page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.r5.xlarge is a memory-optimized virtual machine instance running on the KVM hypervisor. It features 4 dedicated Intel Xeon 8163 processor cores operating at a base frequency of 2.5 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. The instance is configured with 32.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, and it provides a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks indicate that the ecs.r5.xlarge falls into the lower tiers of modern compute capabilities. Single-core and multi-core CPU performance tests, such as stress-ng and Geekbench, consistently place the instance in the bottom 25% of tested hardware. Memory bandwidth tests show a significant drop-off under load, with small-block cached reads reaching 164.23 GB/sec, while large-block uncached reads drop to 20.9 GB/sec, categorized as poor. Cryptographic and compression tasks, including gzip compression and bzip3 decompression, also register in the bottom 10% to 25% performance brackets.

The primary tradeoff of the ecs.r5.xlarge is its high memory capacity relative to its modest processing power. While the 32.0 GB RAM allocation supports memory-intensive operations, the underlying Intel Xeon 8163 CPU limits throughput for compute-heavy tasks. This configuration is qualitatively cost-efficient for workloads requiring a larger memory footprint without demanding high-performance processing. Suitable workloads include caching layers, small-scale database operations, Redis deployments, and memory-bound general-purpose applications that do not require local NVMe storage or high network throughput.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.r5.xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 4 dedicated Intel Xeon 8163 vCPUs running at 2.5 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. It is equipped with 32.0 GB of RAM, providing a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per vCPU, but lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Baseline network bandwidth is rated at 2 Gbps. Benchmark data indicates modest performance, with CPU and memory bandwidth results generally falling into the bottom 25% of tested hardware. Large-block uncached memory reads and single-threaded gzip compression show poor throughput. Consequently, the instance presents a clear tradeoff between high memory capacity and limited processing speed. It is best suited for memory-bound workloads with low compute demands, such as Redis caching, small database operations, and lightweight memory-intensive applications.

tagline: A memory-optimized virtual server offering a high RAM-to-vCPU ratio for memory-intensive workloads with moderate processing demands.

32.0 GB of system memory with 8.0 GB per core
No local storage or GPU hardware included
Suited for Redis caching and small database operations
